title: AssertionSidecar
description: The portable `<media>.loupe.json` assertion sidecar — the SOURCE OF TRUTH for human metadata about a media file (ratings, person names, a deliberate log-profile pick). ASSERTIONS_SIDECAR.md. It is an ordinary JSON file next to the media (same directory, name = media filename + `.loupe.json`), so the truth travels with the bytes on any cross-volume / SMB / USB copy and is readable by a second client with no JuiceMount in the path. JuiceMount's Tier-B `assertions` table is a REBUILDABLE INDEX over these sidecars, never the silo that owns them. Join key = the portable content-hash `asset_key` (xxh3 of size‖head‖tail, the same source_hash already on the wire), with a path/name fallback until bytes are hashed; the JuiceFS inode is a Tier-B accelerator column ONLY, never written here and never identity. An asset with no assertions has NO sidecar (an empty one is never written).

schema.description: Versioned shape tag; bumped only on a breaking change. Forward-compat readers ignore unknown namespaces + unknown top-level keys (hence additionalProperties:true).

asset_key.description: PRIMARY join key — the portable content hash, `xxh3:<16 hex>` (xxh3 of size‖head‖tail = SampleHash). Survives rename/copy/SMB. MAY be empty ("") for a not-yet-hashed asset, in which case the sidecar's location + media_filename is the binding (path/name fallback) until a hash is stamped.

media_filename.description: Basename of the media this sidecar belongs to — the path/name fallback anchor and the human-readable association.

assertions.description: The namespaced (namespace,key,value) triples — verbatim JM-ASSERT wire shape — each carrying its author stamp. LWW-resolved: exactly one triple per (namespace,key); the per-author history (for the deferred author-merge model) lives in the Tier-B index, so merge lands with no sidecar-format migration.

namespace.description: Assertion namespace: "person" | "log_profile" | "rating" | "pick" | "tag" | (future). Unknown namespaces are ignored by forward-compat readers, never an error.

key.description: Namespace-defined key; e.g. "value", or a cluster_id for the "person" namespace.

value.description: The asserted value. null is a RETRACT (the user took their assertion back) and is DISTINCT from a real force-negative value like log_profile "none" (the user asserts it is NOT log).

asserted_by.description: Author identity (e.g. "ol:leland@…/MacBook"). The LWW tiebreak field now and the author-merge unit later; a privacy opt-in toggles per author.

asserted_at.description: RFC-3339 UTC timestamp — the last-writer-wins ordering field.
